channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: sha256sum, shasum, Get-FileHash — and still no good way to compare hashes
item.description: I download a file. The website has a SHA-256 hash next to it. I want to verify it. So I run sha256sum, get a wall of hex, paste the expected hash next to it, and squint at two 64-character strings...

item.title: Why Your AI Agent's Phone Number Gets Blocked (And How to Fix It)
item.description: You built an AI agent that onboards to a new service, creates an account, fills in a form, and gets all the way to SMS verification — then hits a wall. "This phone number cannot be used for...

item.title: Notion Reverse Autopilot - AI That Watches Your Chaos and Builds Order
item.description: This is a submission for the Notion MCP Challenge What I Built Notion Reverse Autopilot - an AI agent that flips the productivity paradigm on its head. Every productivity tool assumes you're...

item.title: Monte Carlo Simulation for Stock Price Forecasting: Historical vs Implied Volatility in Python
item.description: 1. Introduction Forecasting financial markets is a sophisticated fusion of quantitative precision and global economic nuance. In this quest, the Monte Carlo simulation stands out as a premier...

item.title: BASIC QUERIES FOR SQL
item.description: 1. Movies with Rental Rate Greater Than 3 ```sql id="m1a2b3" SELECT * FROM film WHERE rental_rate > 3; This query returns all movies with a rental rate greater than 3. --- ## 2. Movies with Rental...

item.title: I built an AI Second Brain on SurrealDB - and connected it to Claude via MCP
item.description: In my previous posts, I talked about why SurrealDB feels like the Swiss army knife of databases - graph relations, vector search, schemaful tables, and record links all in one engine. I wanted to...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Code and Cowork can control your computer
item.description: Anthropic has updated Claude to perform tasks in its Code and Cowork AI tools autonomously by using your computer for you. The new feature can be used to automatically open files, use web browsers...

item.title: Suspense is a symptom
item.description: This post is part of a series on Inglorious Web. You can start from the beginning, or read it standalone — the argument here is self-contained. Inglorious Web didn't start as a React alternative....

item.title: Share your cancer code!
item.description: Today I was doing a read-through of existing code to prepare for an upcoming feature, and I came across this abomination. # controller code # GET /project_team_tasks def show @teams =...

item.title: Documenting a Narcissist Smear Campaign in Text Messages
item.description: Something feels off. You just received a text from someone you barely know, saying something about you that isn't true. Or maybe it's from a friend who suddenly turned cold, referencing things you...

item.title: The 5-Layer Memory Architecture That Keeps Our AI Agent Running 24/7 Without Forgetting
item.description: Most AI agents have amnesia. You close the chat window. They forget everything. Every session starts from zero. Every context window fills up and dumps the earliest memories to make room for new...

item.title: Ayaneo says selling its Windows gaming handheld ‘is no longer sustainable’
item.description: Ayaneo is suspending preorders for its $1,999 Next 2 gaming handheld due to the skyrocketing price of digital storage. In a post on Monday, Ayaneo says the overall cost to build its Windows...

item.title: Mirage raises $75M to continue building models for its AI video editing app Captions
item.description: Mirage, the maker of video editing app Captions, has raised $75 million in growth financing from General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund (CVF).

item.title: The Hisense U7SG is a great midrange TV you shouldn’t buy yet
item.description: With all of the focus on RGB LED technology, it seemed like more affordable mini-LED TVs had been shooed to the side. But with the Hisense U7SG, we're getting our first great midrange general-use...

item.title: Zoox is bringing its robotaxis to Austin and Miami
item.description: Zoox is stretching out its legs. The Amazon-owned robotaxi today announced that it's gearing up to expand its service in San Francisco and Las Vegas, while also eyeing two additional cities -...

item.title: Nuki adds Apple Home Key to its smart lock
item.description: This week, my top pick for a retrofit smart lock launched the first Aliro-compatible keypad. The Nuki Keypad 2 NFC ($179) brings tap-to-unlock via Apple's Home Key and Samsung's Digital Home Key...

item.title: The Download: tracing AI-fueled delusions, and OpenAI admits Microsoft risks
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The hardest question to answer about AI-fueled delusions...

item.title: Agile Robots becomes the latest robotics company to partner with Google DeepMind
item.description: Agile Robots will incorporate Google DeepMind's robotics foundation models into its bots while collecting data for the AI research lab.

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as...

item.title: Nintendo cuts Switch 2 production amid weakening console sales
item.description: Nintendo will produce fewer Switch 2 units than it previously projected in response to weakening demand for the console in the US, Bloomberg reports. Four million units of Nintendo's flagship...

item.title: 5 Learnings from the First-Ever Gartner Market Guide for Guardian Agents
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Gartner published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, marking an important milestone for this emerging category. For those unfamiliar with the various Gartner...

item.title: Team Mirai and Democracy
item.description: Japan’s election last month and the rise of the country’s newest and most innovative political party, Team Mirai, illustrates the viability of a different way to do politics. In this model,...

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description: Two more GitHub Actions workflows have become the latest to be compromised by credential-stealing malware by a threat actor known as TeamPCP, the cloud-native cybercriminal operation also behind...

item.title: Chris Hayes Has Some Advice for Keeping Up With the News
item.description: The host of MS Now’s All In, knows how hard it is to stay current. But he also knows where you should focus your attention—and it starts with a sober view of AI.

item.title: Your Body Is Betraying Your Right to Privacy
item.description: Attachment to smart devices and biometric surveillance leaves Americans more vulnerable to police searches than ever. Left unchecked it will only get worse.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same...

item.title: U.S. Sentences Russian Hacker to 6.75 Years for Role in $9M Ransomware Damage
item.description: A 26-year-old Russian citizen has been sentenced in the U.S. to 6.75 years (81 months) in prison for his role in assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware crew, in...

item.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
item.description: Citrix has released security updates to address two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway, including a critical flaw that could be exploited to leak sensitive data from the...

item.title: A New Framework for Evaluating Voice Agents (EVA)
The US government just banned consumer routers made outside the US
item.description: In December, the Federal Communications Commission banned all future drones made in foreign countries from being imported into the United States, unless or until their maker gets an exemption....
